data.title: REG - Datalex PLC Datalex PLC - DLE - Alan Dunne appointed Chief Product Officer
data.description:
RNS Number : 4589F Datalex PLC 17 April 2025  Alan Dunne appointed Chief Product Officer

Dublin, Ireland, 17 April 2025: Datalex plc ("Datalex", the "Company" or the "Group") (Euronext Growth Dublin: DLE), a market leader in airline e-commerce solutions, today announces Alan Dunne has joined the Company as Chief Product Officer. He returns to Datalex having previously held positions including Chief Technology Officer and Chief Innovation Officer with the Company.

Alan brings deep industry knowledge and Datalex expertise where he joins from AWS as a Principal Engineer. In his new role as Chief Product Officer, Alan will be instrumental in shaping Datalex's product vision and ensuring that Datalex will continue to play a pivotal role in empowering airlines to thrive as modern retailers.

Commenting on the appointment, Jonathan Rockett, Datalex CEO, said: "It is great to welcome Alan back to Datalex as Chief Product Officer. This is a vital role for the Company and Alan brings significant expertise along with a brilliant track record of being at the forefront of innovation in the airline industry.

Alan joins with a strong understanding of our business and the opportunities that lie ahead for the Company. Alan's leadership will be instrumental in driving product innovation and delivering even greater value to our airline partners."

Alan Dunne, Chief Product Officer commented: "I'm delighted to return to Datalex. It is a pivotal time for the industry and a really exciting time for Datalex. I see huge opportunity ahead for Datalex to evolve through product innovation and become the technology partner of choice for airlines that want full control over their retail offering. I look forward to working with the Datalex team and Datalex's customers to deliver on this opportunity."

 Ends 

About Datalex  

Datalex is a market leader in airline e-commerce technology. Datalex's Stellex product suite, launched in 2024, gives airlines the tools they need to drive revenue and profit as digital retailers. Datalex has a strong track record working with some of the most innovative airline brands worldwide. The Group is headquartered in Dublin, Ireland, and maintains offices across Europe, the Americas, and Asia. Datalex plc is a publicly listed company, on Euronext Growth, Dublin. Learn more at www.datalex.com   

Contact Details:

James Nolan: Datalex@sodali.com or +353 86 089 2395

data.title: BioMerieux Shares Fall After Warning on Currency-Exchange Hit
data.description:
By Anthony O. Goriainoff

Shares in bioMerieux fell after the biotechnology company warned that it anticipates a larger currency-exchange hit than previously expected this year.

The diagnostics equipment maker on Thursday said it expects a hit of around 35 million to 40 million euros ($39.9 million to $45.6 million) to 2025 adjusted earnings before interest and taxes, having previously guided for a negative currency effect of around 30 million euros.

BioMerieux said it was confident in achieving at least 10% growth in adjusted EBIT, which strips out exceptional and other one-off items.

The company said it is closely monitoring the evolving tariff situation, assessing its financial effects and exploring mitigating actions, it said. BioMerieux is mainly exposed to U.S. tariffs on the 15% of finished goods imported into the country, as well as some Chinese and European raw-material imports and components. It is also exposed to tariffs on imports from the U.S. to China, which accounts for around 5% of group sales, BioMerieux said.

The company said consolidated sales rose 14% in the first quarter to 1.1 billion euros, and backed its sales guidance for the year of at least 7% growth at constant exchange rates.

Shares in early European trade were down 4.7% at 112.60 euros.

Write to Anthony O. Goriainoff at anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com

data.title: N. Macedonia's Replek to pay 6.5 euro/share dividend for 2024
data.description:
SKOPJE (N. Macedonia), April 17 (SeeNews) - North Macedonia-based drug maker Replek [MSE:REPL] said on Thursday its shareholders have approved a plan to distribute a gross dividend of 400 denars ($7.4/6.5 euro) per share for 2024.

Replek will distribute a total of 95.2 million denars in dividend from last year's profit, the company said in a bourse filing.

Replek made a consolidated net profit of 224.2 million denars in 2024, down 20% on the year. Last year, the pharmaceutical company carried out a 1 to 10 stock split without changing the company's capital.

For 2023, the company distributed a gross dividend of 3,000 denars per share.

Replek's shares last changed hands on the Macedonian Stock Exchange (MSE) at an average price of 15,821 denars on Thursday, bourse data shows.

In December, Replek joined the Skopje bourse blue-chip index, MBI-10.

(1 euro = 61.58 denars)

data.title: K.P. Energy Commissions 23.1MW Wind Power Capacity at Bhavnagar, India Hybrid Power Poject
data.description:
K.P. Energy has commissioned 23.1MW of wind power capacity for Aditya Birla Renewables Energy at a wind-solar hybrid power project at Bhavnagar in Gujarat, India, according to a BSE filing on Thursday.

The capacity is part of the wind-solar hybrid power project comprising 23.1 MW wind and 25.69 MWDC solar.

The commissioned wind capacity consists of 11 wind turbine generators connected to a 140 MW power evacuation facility at the Fulsar site in Bhavnagar for Aditya Birla Renewables.

data.title: SeABank reports profit before tax of US$168.14 million in Q1/2025
data.description:
SeABank reports profit before tax of US$168.14 million in Q1/2025

PR Newswire

HANOI, Vietnam, April 17, 2025

HANOI, Vietnam, April 17, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — Southeast Asia Commercial Joint Stock Bank (SeABank, stock code: SSB) announced its consolidated first-quarter results 2025 with positive figures, reflecting robust performance.

Strong performance exceeds Q1/2025 KPIs

SeABank has delivered outstanding results in Q1/2025, sustaining growth momentum compared to the same period in 2024, particularly: PBT reached VND4,350 billion (US$168.14 million), fulfilling 269% of KPIs and marking a sharp YoY increase of nearly 189%. TOI hit VND5,820 billion (US$224.96 million), achieving 184% of KPIs and increasing by over 115% YoY. A standout contributor to this income growth was NoII, which surged to VND3,369 billion (US$130.22 million) — an increase of 378%, completing 340% of KPIs. Those positive results were driven by SeABank's proactive expansion of non-credit services, effective cost control, enhanced risk management, and the implementation of strategic projects.

By the end of Q1/2025, total mobilization from customer deposits and valuable papers at SeABank reached VND189,993 billion (US$7.34 billion). Total lending balance was VND213,048 billion (US$8.24 billion) - a net year-on-year increase of VND3,693 billion (US$142.75 million).

Additionally, SeABank's NPL ratio slightly declined to 1.84%, reflecting effective credit risk management amid ongoing macroeconomic challenges and a rising NPL trend across the banking sector. The debt coverage ratio was maintained at 81.81%, ensuring sufficient provision in line with regulatory requirements.

As of March 31st, 2025, SeABank's total assets reached VND333,746 billion (US$12.9 billion), up 2.47% - equivalent to a net increase of VND8,047 billion (US$311.04 million) from December 31st, 2024; charter capital reached VND 28,450 billion (US$1.1 billion).

Enhancing international partnerships, expanding funding and supporting SME

In Q1/2025, SeABank continued its position as a trusted partner of international financial institutions to promote capital access for Vietnamese enterprises, thereby strengthening capacity and advancing focus on key business sectors.

By the end of March 2025, SeABank's total international mobilization reached nearly US$1.1 billion, following an investment of US$80 million from the French Development Finance Institution (Proparco) and the Dutch Entrepreneurial Development Bank (FMO). This not only strengthens SeABank's funding base to support SMEs and women-owned businesses, but also affirms the Bank's reputation, operational efficiency, and effective capital utilization.

Besides, in Q1/2025, SeABank has completed transferring 100% of its stake in Post and Telecommunication Finance Company (PTF) to AEON Financial Service. This helped enhance the Bank's capital base and financial capacity for scale expansion, technological investments, while driving sustainable business growth with strategic focus.

data.title: Eurasia Mining Says Still Committed to Selling Russian Assets
data.description:
Eurasia Mining said Thursday it remains committed to ultimately selling its Russian assets, but noted that there was no guarantee of finalizing an agreement.

The miner said it is continuing limited activities at the assets, which include the West Kytlim operating mine, the Monchetundra project mining license, the NKT brownfield project, and the Nyud brownfield project entitlement, to keep them in an optimal condition for a possible sale.

On 17 April 2025, the Company allotted 391,947 new Ordinary Shares pursuant to the Offer conditional only on Admission (the timetable for which is set out in more detail below).

The offer price at which the 391,947 new Ordinary Shares were allotted was 34.82 pence per Ordinary Share, which was calculated, in accordance with the terms of the Offer, by reference to the net asset value of an Ordinary Share on 11 April 2025 (as announced on 14 April 2025, being the last published net asset value per Ordinary Share) divided by 0.965 (to allow for the costs of the Offer).

Application will shortly be made for the 391,947 new Ordinary Shares to be admitted to the premium segment of the Official List of the Financial Conduct Authority and to trading on the premium segment of London Stock Exchange plc’s main market for listed securities ("Admission"). When issued, the new Ordinary Shares will rank pari passu with the existing Ordinary Shares.

As a result of the issue, the total number of Ordinary Shares in issue will be 372,843,202 with each Ordinary Share carrying one vote each.  Therefore, the total voting rights in the Company will be 372,843,202. data.title: Albanian banks' net profit rises 9% y/y in Jan-Feb
data.description:
TIRANA (Albania), April 17 (SeeNews) - The net profit of Albanian banks rose to 6.87 billion leks ($78.6 million/69.1 million euro) in the first two months of 2025, from 6.3 billion leks reported for the same period last year, the Albanian Association of Banks (AAB) said.

The outstanding loan portfolio of banks in Albania totalled 848.7 billion leks at the end of February, increasing from 745.2 billion leks a year earlier, AAB said in a monthly report earlier this week, quoting Albanian central bank data.

Deposits in Albanian banks increased to 1,735 billion leks as at the end of February, from 1,620 billion leks a year earlier.

The total assets of the country's banking system grew to 2,136 billion leks as of end-February, from 1,981 billion leks at the end of February 2024.

The figures comprise the financial results of eleven Albanian banks. Banka Kombetare Tregtare (BKT) was the largest among them in terms of assets at the end of 2024.

(1 euro = 99.4 Albanian leks)

The stock, already listed on BSE, will trade on NSE under the ticker symbol "CIFL".

data.title: Romania's 7X Partners acquires 40% stake in Leventer Medical
data.description:
BUCHAREST (Romania), April 17 (SeeNews) - Romanian private equity firm 7X Partners has acquired a 40% stake in Leventer Medical, the local healthcare group said.

The investment, whose value was not disclosed, will help Leventer Medical develop a private oncology hospital and equip it with state-of-the-art technologies, the healthcare group said in a statement on Tuesday.

"The new hospital will have, in the first stage, an area of ​​approximately 10,000 square meters and will include 6 operating rooms and 76 beds, an outpatient service with 18 offices and treatment rooms, imaging and medical oncology departments, as well as a pathological anatomy laboratory. In the second stage, we aim to increase the area to 12,000 square meters, as well as the capacity – up to 100 beds, but also the development of a superficial radiotherapy department," Leventer Medical Group co-founder and shareholder Claudiu Opran said.

Local business news website Profit.ro reported on Tuesday that Leventer Medical Group will launch Baneasa Tumor Center Hospital in autumn 2025, following a total investment of over 25 million euro ($28.4 million).

Leventer Medical initially announced the development of a dermatology and oncological surgery private hospital in Bucharest in 2022, with an investment estimated at 10 million euro.

Established in 2012, Leventer Medical Group currently operates three dermatology clinics in Bucharest.

($ = 0.8801 euro)

data.title: Camellia Unit Enters Nonbinding Deal to Sell Indian Tea Estate
data.description:
Camellia's 74%-owned Goodricke Group signed a nonbinding memorandum of understanding for the proposed sale of its Leesh River Tea Estate in Western Dooars, India.

The transaction is still subject to due diligence procedures, the British agriculture group said Thursday.

Goodricke intends to sell the asset to Sombaria Agri Products Private Ltd., according to a Wednesday filing at the Bombay Stock Exchange.

data.title: European shares drop as investors gauge earnings ahead of ECB meeting
data.description:
Key points:
  • ECB decision at 1215 GMT, Lagarde press conference at 1245 GMT
  • Pernod misses sales Q3 forecasts; down 0.5%
  • Hermes Q1 sales miss forecast; down 2.2%

European shares fell on Thursday as investors parsed corporate earnings to gauge the fallout of U.S. President Donald Trump's erratic trade plans, while awaiting European Central Bank's policy decision later in the day.

The pan-European STOXX 600 index fell 0.5%, as of 0818 GMT, but remained on track for a 4% climb this week.

Other regional indexes - Germany , France , Spain , and the UK CURRENCYCOM:UK100 fell between 0.4% and 0.6%.

France's Hermes dropped 2.2% after the Birkin bag maker posted a rare quarterly sales miss, joining rival LVMH , which also reported sales below expectations earlier this week.

Spirits maker Pernod Ricard dipped 0.5% after reporting a fall third-quarter sales, partly hurt by tariffs.

Analysts have pared their forecasts for European corporate profitability as tit-for-tat tariffs sparked by U.S. President Donald Trump's multi-front trade war have dimmed the global growth outlook, triggering market volatility reminiscent of the early days of COVID-19.

The European benchmark index is down about 10% from its March record closing high.

The ECB's rate decision is scheduled for 1215 GMT, with markets seeing a 25 basis point rate cut as all-but-certain to cushion a struggling economy from tariff uncertainty.

Investors will sift through ECB President Christine Lagarde's comments at a 1245 GMT press conference for hints on futures rate cuts.

"It's too early for the ECB to really go much more aggressive on the dovish stance. So we do expect cuts and a pretty balanced communication but we don't think they will over-deliver on what the market is pricing in terms of rate cuts and guidance," said Emmanuel Cau, head of European equity strategy at Barclays.

"The market wants to see some progress on trade deals, whether it's China, Europe or Japan and that will be more important than anything else."

U.S. Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell acknowledged U.S. economic growth appears to be slowing, but added that the Fed would wait for more economic data before changing interest rates, causing a selloff on Wall Street selloff.

Investors also shied away from making big bets ahead of a four-day-long weekend on account of Good Friday and Easter Monday.

Siemens Energy jumped 10.5% after the German energy group raised its outlook for the current fiscal year after posting its best profit margin since being spun off from former parent Siemens AG.
